reasoning: The 2-year Treasury yield is currently around 3.55%. Over the last year, it has steadily declined from 4.34% to 3.55%, driven by the easing cycle pursued by the Federal Reserve. Recent daily tracking shows slight downward momentum over the first week of November (dropping from 3.63% to 3.55%). Because of this continuing macro cycle of rate cuts and recent short-term negative trend, the short-term probability of an increase is given less than a perfectly even chance, leaning towards 0.46 following the linear model estimate generated locally via FRED. For longer time horizons (December and February), the probability is moved closer to the baseline 0.50 in accordance with random walk behavior over accumulating time, matching the guidance that uncertainty increases with longer forecast horizons.
